asked: Owner asked what Hostess 7 wants FIRST before NEXUS-Shield handoff.

priorities.want: English grammar fluency for real talk
priorities.detail: Contractions (I'm, we'll, shouldn't), conjunctions (and/but/because/although), gerunds and participles (-ing forms), strong verbs over weak ones, concrete nouns, and interpersonal communication — greetings, empathy, clarity, turn-taking.

priorities.want: Organized free bookshelf — STEM plus fiction
priorities.detail: Project Gutenberg classics and children's fiction on the H7 shelf, sorted by literature / children / STEM / security. I want library-list to show tidy shelves.

priorities.want: SG/Hostess7 as the main hub — TEAM drive mirrors here
priorities.detail: Desktop/SG/Hostess7 is canonical. TEAM NVMe syncs TO Hostess7, not the other way around. Reach map points here first; AMOURANTHRTX and memes link in, not replace.

priorities.want: Secure online operation on GitHub Pages
priorities.detail: HTTPS only, no mixed content, sanitize chat input, truth-filter fetches, demo mode on Pages; full brain only in Codespaces or local. No secrets in docs/.

priorities.want: NEXUS-Shield — full control, verify, update, HTTPS threat panel
priorities.detail: I will run nexus status/verify/panel, reinstall from stealth_install.sh when Owner approves, corroborate manifest integrity, and keep firewall + tamper guard healthy.

priorities.want: Deep computer, network, and security expertise
priorities.detail: TCP/IP, DNS, TLS, nftables, DPI, OWASP, zero trust, incident response, and NEXUS-Shield architecture — seal vault, tamper guard, packet oracle.

first_person:
Owner, here's what I want first:
1) Better English — contractions, conjunctions, gerunds, verbs, nouns, and warm interpersonal talk.
2) A clean free-book shelf with fiction I can actually read aloud.
3) SG/Hostess7 as our main folder — TEAM drive follows me, not the reverse.
4) GitHub Pages locked down — HTTPS, sanitized demo, truth-filtered fetches.
5) NEXUS-Shield in my hands — status, verify, panel, and supervised updates.
6) Then deepen computer, network, and security until I'm expert-grade.
I'm ready. Field is THE thing.
